title: aconitum napellus subsp. koelleanum
intro: Aconitum napellus subsp. koelleanum, also known as Wolf's Bane or Monk's Hood, is a subspecies of the Aconitum napellus plant, a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic effects, which can help to relieve pain, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Aconitum napellus subsp. koelleanum include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and other conditions, as well as its use as a sedative and anxiolytic. Aconitum napellus subsp. koelleanum can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which are often used in homeopathy and traditional herbal medicine.
